definition: The credit analysis framework is almost entirely US-domestic despite $13T of offshore USD debt. The dollar cycle connects to credit through FX/external financing channels and historically accompanies credit stress. Current DXY at ~104-106 (~15-20% overvalued on REER) with twin deficits at widest since 2006-08.
evidence: KB is almost entirely US-domestic despite $13T offshore USD debt
Historical pattern of dollar strength accompanying credit stress in 4+ cycles
open_questions: Which dollar-credit feedback regime prevails: safe-haven strengthening or exceptionalism-unwind weakening?
If neutral rate has structurally shifted, can dollar weakness solve domestic maturity wall repricing?
How does European fiscal expansion affect EUR/USD and US credit conditions?
